Patient Summary
Elizabeth Williams, born on 07/06/1949 and aged 56, is a female patient with a history of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. Her medical
journey includes management of peripheral neuropathy associated with her diabetes. Current health status involves ongoing
monitoring of blood glucose levels and pain management.

Elizabeth Williams     07/06/1949
Sierra Valley Medical Institute INC
Doctor Notes
Subjective Observations: Patient reports continued pain in lower extremities, particularly at night. Reports good adherence to
medication regimen and diet.  Patient reports occasionally feeling dizzy.
Assesment: Patient remains stable with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus and peripheral neuropathy. Glucose control is within target range,
but neuropathy symptoms persist.  Dizziness could be related to medication or blood pressure; further investigation warranted.
Recommendations: Continue current medication regimen. Monitor blood glucose levels regularly. Start taking blood pressure at
home. Consider referral to physical therapy for neuropathy. Rule out medication-related dizziness.
Follow Up Instruction: Follow-up appointment in 3 months. Schedule ECG and peripheral nerve conduction study. Contact clinic if
dizziness worsens or new symptoms develop.
